title: Code Review Result
description: Provider-agnostic structured output of the code-review skill.

reportLanguage.description: Language for prose fields and the Markdown report. Enum values elsewhere in this schema always stay English regardless of this setting.

scope.description: Present when fewer than all changed files were reviewed. Omit when coverage was complete.

exclusions.description: Path globs excluded, with the reason, e.g. 'docs/** — excluded at requester's direction'.

verdict.description: Derived from findings: any blocking -> changes_required; warnings only -> pass_with_warnings; none -> pass; review aborted -> unable_to_complete.

summary.description: One to three sentences stating what was reviewed and the outcome.

base.description: Base ref or commit sha, when applicable.

head.description: Head ref or commit sha, when applicable.

inferred.description: True when no explicit target was supplied and the target was inferred from repository state.

instructionsRead.description: Repository-relative paths of instruction files actually read. Do not list files that were skipped.

detectedStacks.description: Free-form stack labels detected from repository files and changed paths, e.g. 'ASP.NET Core', 'Angular', 'Go', 'SQL', 'Docker'.

checks.description: Deterministic checks considered. A status of 'passed' means the command was executed and exited successfully.

name.description: The command as invoked, e.g. 'dotnet test'.

details.description: Relevant output excerpt or the reason for skipped/not_available.

findings.description: Ordered most severe first. Empty is a valid result.

file.description: Repository-relative path. Null only when the finding is not tied to a file.

title.description: Short noun phrase naming the concrete problem. One line.

message.description: What is wrong. At most two sentences. Never describes how the problem was found — that belongs in evidence.

impact.description: The concrete consequence if this ships. At most two sentences.

suggestion.description: Practical remediation, specific enough to act on. At most three sentences.

evidence.description: Optional proof of the claim: a non-obvious command, a byte-level check, a count across files. At most two sentences. Omit when the cited line speaks for itself.

patch.description: Concrete before/after for the fix, one entry per edit site. Deliberately exempt from the prose length caps — code is not prose, and truncating it would defeat the point. Include whenever the remediation is a specific code edit; omit when the fix is structural or a judgement call. Code is never translated: only the surrounding prose follows reportLanguage.

file.description: Repository-relative path of this edit site.

before.description: Current code, verbatim from the file. Omit for a pure addition.

after.description: Proposed replacement. Must be real code, not a description of code.

note.description: Optional one-line caption when the edit is not self-explanatory.

rule.description: Applicable repository rule, e.g. 'AGENTS.md#layering'. Null when only general policy applies.

confidence.description: Optional. 'verified' means confirmed by reading the surrounding code or by a failing check.

preExisting.description: The problem is not introduced by this change. Only committed secrets qualify (see policies/security.md); a pre-existing finding may never be blocking.

previouslyDismissed.description: Required when re-reporting something an earlier review deliberately dismissed: what changed to justify reversing that judgement. review-pr refuses to render a silent reversal.

fp.description: Optional stable fingerprint for cross-round matching. Defaults to '<category>:<file>'.

limitations.description: Anything that prevented full review: checks not run, files unreadable, missing infrastructure. Required to be non-empty when verdict is unable_to_complete.

dismissed.description: Things examined and deliberately not reported, with the reason. Carried into the next review's state so a later round cannot silently reverse the judgement. Record anything a reader might otherwise expect to see flagged.

fp.description: Fingerprint, '<category>:<file>'. Derived from category and file when omitted.

why.description: Why this is not a finding. A later round must address this reason to report it.

report.description: Optional Markdown rendering of this result, for direct display to a developer.
